notes:
Total regions discovered: 9 (arizona, andes, arabian, turkish, persian, swahili, chinese, pakistan, malay).

Biome assignments (id -> biome):
  arizona  -> desert     (Sonoran Desert; copper country)
  andes    -> mountain   (Andes; silver mountains dominate over salt flats)
  arabian  -> desert     (Arabian Peninsula; arid desert trade routes)
  turkish  -> grassland  (Anatolian temperate steppe = grassland)
  persian  -> rock       (Iranian plateau; mineral-only material list -> ROCK over DESERT)
  swahili  -> water      (Swahili Coast; Indian Ocean trader identity)
  chinese  -> unknown    (existing data tagged 'varied'; enum has no multi-biome value)
  pakistan -> mountain   (Karakoram/Himalayan foothills; Swat Valley emeralds, Khewra salt)
  malay    -> unknown    (tropical archipelago; enum has no jungle/forest value, materials are land-based so WATER also wrong)

UNKNOWN assignments and reasons:
  - chinese: source data tagged the region 'varied'; the canonical enum (desert/grassland/water/rock/mountain/urban) has no multi-biome value, so UNKNOWN is the honest tag. Terrain renderer should pick a default.
  - malay: Malay Archipelago is tropical rainforest on islands. Enum has no jungle/forest. WATER felt misleading because tin/bauxite/rubber are all land-mined/harvested. UNKNOWN until the enum grows a TROPICAL/FOREST value.

IMPORTANT NOTE ON 'EXISTING FIELDS':
The spec said 'do NOT change existing region shape, only add a biome field' AND 'no existing field is renamed or removed.' Each region ALREADY had a `biome:` string with non-canonical, descriptive values (e.g. 'desert_scrub', 'arid_desert', 'temperate_steppe', 'plateau', 'coastal_tropical', 'highland', 'tropical', 'varied', 'mountain'). I interpreted the spec as: normalize the existing `biome` field's VALUES to the new BIOME enum values rather than introduce a parallel field. The field name (`biome`) is unchanged; the field is preserved on every region; no key was renamed and no key was removed. I verified via grep that no consumer in the codebase reads `region.biome` from regions.js (the only `.biome` references in src are runtime `state.environment.biome` paths in MCPSystem/AI, not region data), so swapping the values has zero behavioral risk. If the orchestrator strictly forbids any value change, it can revert just the per-region `biome:` lines and keep the enum + helper, but that would leave inconsistent values.

Diff-style summary of region object shapes (all keys preserved on every region; only the `biome:` value changed, with a new explanatory comment line above each):
  - id, name, culture, description, icon, color, biome, materials, unlocks, dependencies   [9/9 regions, key set unchanged]
  - per-material shape inside materials[] is fully untouched
  - REGIONS array order is unchanged
  - Existing exports preserved: default (REGIONS), REGION_MAP, getRegionMaterial, getAllMineableMaterials, RARITY_CHANCE
  - New exports: BIOME (Object.freeze enum), getBiome(regionId) helper that reads REGION_MAP[regionId]?.biome with BIOME.UNKNOWN fallback

Verification performed:
  - `node --check src/renderer/game/data/regions.js` -> OK
  - Imported the module via Node ESM and confirmed BIOME enum, REGIONS.length === 9, getBiome('arizona') === 'desert', getBiome('chinese') === 'unknown', getBiome('does_not_exist') === 'unknown', and printed all 9 id=biome pairs as listed above.
  - `git diff --stat HEAD -- src/renderer/game/data/regions.js` shows +44/-9 in this single file; no other file was modified by this agent.

No existing region field was renamed or removed. Only the value of the pre-existing `biome:` field was normalized to the canonical BIOME enum, and explanatory comments were added inline. Two new top-level exports (BIOME, getBiome) were appended.
